name: PAY_CIR_DATA_BACKUP_GLB_TRX
description: This table backup cir data whose effective start date is after global transfer date for later processing.

columns.Name: CIR_DATA_BACKUP_GLB_TRX_ID
columns.Comments: Table surrogate key id CIR_DATA_BACKUP_GLB_TRX_ID

columns.Name: MASS_ACTION_HEADER_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier for the mass global transfer batch. To be populated during copy future phase.

columns.Name: MASS_ACTION_LINE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier for the mass global transfer line (person level). To be populated during copy future phase.

columns.Name: SOURCE_PAYROLL_RELATIONSHIP_ID
columns.Comments: Payroll Relationship Id of the source

columns.Name: ENTITY_NAME
columns.Comments: This would be the table name e.g. PAY_DIR_CARDS_F, PAY_DIR_CARD_COMPONENTS_F etc

columns.Name: SOURCE_SURROGATE_KEY_ID
columns.Comments: This is the surrogate key ID i.e. dir_card_id, dir_card_comp_id etc of the source (from where we are copying the data)

columns.Name: EFFECTIVE_START_DATE
columns.Comments: Date Effective Entity: indicates the date at the beginning of the date range within which the row is effective.

columns.Name: EFFECTIVE_END_DATE
columns.Comments: Date Effective Entity: indicates the date at the end of the date range within which the row is effective.

columns.Name: ACTION_TYPE
columns.Comments: Possible values are:  CREATE, UPDATE (i.e. date effective update)

columns.Name: ATTRIBUTE_NAME
columns.Comments: Name of the attribute that is being stored e.g. DIR_CARD_COMP_DEF_ID.  If new physical record but no change in any attribute from previous record then store "DUMMY" here.

columns.Name: OLD_VALUE_CHAR
columns.Comments: If attribute is char, then store the old value of the attribute here in case of both INSERT and INSERT_UPDATE (i.e. date effective update)

columns.Name: NEW_VALUE_CHAR
columns.Comments: If attribute is char, then store the new value of the attribute here in case of INSERT_UPDATE

columns.Name: OLD_VALUE_NUMBER
columns.Comments: If attribute is number, then store the old value of the attribute here in case of both INSERT and INSERT_UPDATE (i.e. date effective update)

columns.Name: NEW_VALUE_NUMBER
columns.Comments: If attribute is number, then store the new value of the attribute here in case of INSERT_UPDATE

columns.Name: OLD_VALUE_DATE
columns.Comments: If attribute is date, then store the old value of the attribute here in case of both INSERT and INSERT_UPDATE (i.e. date effective update)

columns.Name: NEW_VALUE_DATE
columns.Comments: If attribute is date, then store the new value of the attribute here in case of INSERT_UPDATE

columns.Name: DEST_PAYROLL_RELATIONSHIP_ID
columns.Comments: Target Payroll Relationship Id. To be populated during copy future phase.

columns.Name: DESTINATION_SURROGATE_KEY_ID
columns.Comments: This is the surrogate key ID i.e. dir_card_id, dir_card_comp_id etc of the target (where we are copying the data to).  If the card, component is being re-used it could be the same id as the source.  To be populated during copy future phase.

columns.Name: COPY_STATUS
columns.Comments: This represents the status of future copy to destination (i.e. during copyFutureCIRChangesForGLBTransfer). Possible values are SUCCESS, FAILURE. To be populated during copy future phase.

columns.Name: COPY_INFORMATION
columns.Comments: Log messages that give more information about the copy status. To be populated during copy future phase.

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: ENTERPRISE_ID. Flag used to mark obsolete data when upgrading the seed data in customer pod

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.
